executive_summary: Develop a 2D top-down cyberpunk room environment featuring a core mechanic where players can invert the directional gravity using the spacebar, impacting player movement and physics-enabled objects.

allocations.technical_dept: Scene Configuration: Initialize a new 2D scene with a top-down camera projection. Configure a physics world suitable for 2D top-down gameplay. Implement a player character as a KinematicBody2D or RigidBody2D. Terminal Execution: Implement input detection for the 'spacebar' key. On key press, toggle the Y-axis gravity vector for the player and any physics-enabled environmental objects from positive (downward) to negative (upward) and vice-versa. Ensure player movement (WASD/Arrow keys) adapts correctly to the current gravity direction, allowing movement along 'floor' and 'ceiling' surfaces as they become the primary gravitational pull points. Integrate collision detection for player-environment interactions, especially when gravity flips.
allocations.design_dept: Game Mechanics: Define the precise behavior of gravity inversion; pressing the 'spacebar' should instantly flip the Y-axis gravity's direction. The player should immediately accelerate in the new gravitational direction until a new 'floor' (or 'ceiling') is contacted. Player movement inputs (horizontal) should remain consistent relative to the player's orientation, even when gravity is inverted. Visual/Audio Feedback: Propose specific visual cues (e.g., subtle screen rotation, color shift, particle effects, UI indicator) and an audio effect to clearly communicate the gravity flip to the player. Level Layout: Design a basic cyberpunk-themed room layout. Include static wall boundaries and designated 'floor' and 'ceiling' collision surfaces. Coordinates and Node Tracking: Establish a consistent coordinate system (e.g., (0,0) at the center of the room). Track 'PlayerCharacter' node for position and state. Track 'Wall_North', 'Wall_South', 'Wall_East', 'Wall_West' nodes as static collision boundaries. Track 'Floor_Surface' and 'Ceiling_Surface' nodes as potential gravitational anchors.
allocations.asset_dept: Sprite Specifications: Create a top-down player character sprite ('player_cyber_topdown.png', e.g., 32x32 pixels). Design distinct tileable sprites for cyberpunk floor ('floor_cyber_tile.png', e.g., 16x16 pixels), ceiling ('ceiling_cyber_tile.png', e.g., 16x16 pixels), and wall ('wall_cyber_tile.png', e.g., 16x16 pixels) elements. Optionally, include a few static cyberpunk prop sprites (e.g., 'crate_cyber.png', 'terminal_cyber.png'). Audio Specifications: Source or create a short, impactful sound effect for the 'gravity_flip_sfx.wav'. Provide a looping background music track with a dark, atmospheric cyberpunk theme ('cyberpunk_ambient_loop.ogg'). Validations: All sprite assets must be provided in PNG format with transparent backgrounds. All audio assets must be 44.1kHz, 16-bit stereo, WAV or OGG format.
